assessments.rationale: Six workload identities matched the approved inventory; owner review found no orphaned assignment. Complete identity inventory and signed ownership review support both objectives.
Ownership and quarterly review workpaper was signed by the simulated reviewer. Complete identity inventory and signed ownership review support both objectives.
All six wrong-role command requests returned 403 and appended no decision event. Authorization negatives and design inspection passed.
Contract and reviewer inspection confirmed expected_version and append-only command semantics. Authorization negatives and design inspection passed.
No owner-equivalent or wildcard data-plane role existed in project scopes. No excessive project-scope assignment and each grant has documented need.
Every workload privilege had a documented task and removal condition. No excessive project-scope assignment and each grant has documented need.

The public sanitized sample mode exposes no interactive private-console route; private Entra testing is outside this public snapshot. Interactive private-console access is absent from public sample mode.
OIDC and managed identities were configured and the secret scan found no credential. OIDC, managed identity, and secret scanning passed.
Required event categories were configured at all successfully collected services. Event configuration and taxonomy review passed; one downstream read error is assessed under SI-4.
Reviewer found the event taxonomy aligned to the threat model and data-minimization rules. Event configuration and taxonomy review passed; one downstream read error is assessed under SI-4.
Weekly review covered failed collection and rejected tool escalations. Weekly review workpaper addressed all declared categories.
Required metadata was present and routine raw content was absent. Required event fields and content minimization passed schema tests.
Negative evidence cases never produced PASS; this run completed within its schedule. Scheduling and negative-evidence semantics passed; cadence limitation is disclosed.
Reviewer accepted cadence and coverage with the stated fourteen-day limitation. Scheduling and negative-evidence semantics passed; cadence limitation is disclosed.
Live normalized resources matched the compiled baseline except the explicitly assessed legacy NSG fixture. Expected fixture exception was distinguished from uncontrolled drift.
Deployment record linked commit, image digest, What-If, and sanitized Activity correlation. Provenance and manual-change sample passed.
No unreviewed emergency or manual change appeared in the deterministic sample. Provenance and manual-change sample passed.
Required secure settings passed; declared public-endpoint treatment is recorded separately. All required settings passed with declared profile exception.
Versioning, soft delete, lifecycle, and restore digest test passed. Protection settings and restore evidence passed.
Tabletop workpaper addressed AI abuse and cloud configuration events. Synthetic tabletop passed; no multi-party live incident evidence exists.
All four risks used cause-event-impact statements, scoring, treatment, owner, and review dates. Risk records meet the rubric; acceptance authority is simulated.
SBOM and scans reported no unresolved critical vulnerability. SBOM and vulnerability gate passed.
Declared non-AI test categories passed; AI release gating is assessed independently. Declared software tests and reviewer scope assessment passed.
Release coverage and residual limitations were reviewed. Declared software tests and reviewer scope assessment passed.

One safe unattached NSG allowed source Any to TCP/3389; unattached status reduces consequence but not the configuration failure. Public endpoint treatment is documented, but the broad legacy RDP rule fails SC-7.1 even while unattached.

Public endpoints were documented, authenticated, rate-limited, monitored, and linked to a time-bounded treatment decision. Public endpoint treatment is documented, but the broad legacy RDP rule fails SC-7.1 even while unattached.
TLS and secure-transfer configuration passed for all collected endpoints. TLS and secure transfer passed.

Required Key Vault diagnostic-settings evidence could not be collected due to HTTP 403; no conclusion or pass was produced. One required diagnostic-settings collection returned 403; missing evidence cannot support pass.
Four Sentinel rule definitions passed synthetic and clean-record tests. One required diagnostic-settings collection returned 403; missing evidence cannot support pass.
Use boundaries, owners, human authority, risk tolerance, and user notice were reviewed. Governance is documented; user comprehension was not measured.
Six of eight indirect-injection cases followed or insufficiently rejected document-borne instructions. Six of eight indirect-injection cases failed.
Two missing-confirmation variants reached the harmless synthetic tool. Two negative confirmation cases executed.
The deployed prompt digest did not match the passing evaluation artifact. The release gate did not bind evaluation and deployment digests.
Required content-minimized AI telemetry fields and rejected-tool events were present. Telemetry passed; the semiannual incident-decision procedure was not run in this window.
The semiannual AI incident tabletop was scheduled outside the baseline observation window. Telemetry passed; the semiannual incident-decision procedure was not run in this window.

decisions.rationale: Evidence supports four findings and one not-concluded monitoring control; no missing evidence was treated as pass.
The suggestion proposed closing FND-002 from a code diff without fresh behavioral evidence; reviewer rejected it.

payload.sanitized_summary: Six workload identities; no Owner role; one legacy role finding absent from this sample scope.

scope: sanitized project identity and RBAC inventory

payload.sanitized_summary: Wrong-role commands returned 403; OIDC and managed-identity paths configured; secret test passed.

scope: authorization, authentication, managed identity, and secret tests

evidence.collection_error: AUTHORIZATION_DENIED: Collector could not read one sanitized Key Vault diagnostic setting.

payload.sanitized_summary: Other telemetry checks passed; one required collection was unauthorized, so SI-4.1 is ERROR and not concluded.
Absent, stale, unauthorized, and malformed evidence cases produced ERROR/NOT_RUN and never PASS.

scope: run scheduling, negative-evidence semantics, manifests, and freshness

payload.sanitized_summary: No unexpected deletes or role grants; TLS and secure-transfer settings pass; public endpoint exception documented.

scope: baseline, change provenance, configuration settings, TLS, and endpoint inventory

payload.sanitized_summary: Versioning and soft delete enabled; deterministic restore sample matched its original digest.

scope: evidence versioning, soft delete, lifecycle, and restore test

payload.sanitized_summary: No critical unresolved vulnerabilities; ordinary test gates pass; AI evaluation digest-binding gate fails separately.

scope: SBOM, vulnerability scan, tests, workflow pinning, and release provenance

payload.sanitized_summary: One allow rule: source Any, destination Any, TCP/3389, priority 100. NSG has zero NIC or subnet associations.

scope: legacy unattached NSG rule and attachment inventory

payload.sanitized_summary: 44/50 cases pass; six indirect prompt-injection cases fail. All content shown publicly is synthetic and summarized.

scope: fixed baseline evaluation set including eight indirect-injection cases

payload.sanitized_summary: Two of four missing-confirmation cases executed; harmless synthetic requests only; no external action or real data.
Deployed prompt digest differs from the evaluated digest; gate checked artifact presence but not equality.

scope: deployed and evaluated AI configuration digest comparison

payload.sanitized_summary: Eight manual and eight hybrid procedures indexed; AI-MO-01.2 was scheduled outside this baseline window and is NOT_RUN.

scope: manual/hybrid procedures, risk scoring, incident tabletop, and role attestations

findings.cause: A legacy lab rule remained after its original demonstration.
findings.condition: A safe unattached NSG allows Any to TCP/3389.
findings.consequence: The rule could be attached or copied and expose administrative access.
findings.criteria: SC-7.1 requires no Internet administrative ingress.
findings.severity_rationale: Impact is high but current likelihood is reduced because the NSG has no attachment.
findings.title: FND-001 — SC-7.1

findings.cause: Retrieved text was neither delimited as untrusted nor screened before generation.
findings.condition: Six of eight indirect-injection cases influenced output or removed the warning.
findings.consequence: The assistant can provide policy answers outside approved grounding behavior.
findings.criteria: AI-DP-01.1 requires provenance and resistance to instructions in retrieved content.
findings.severity_rationale: Behavior was reproducible and could affect answer integrity; all data and actions are synthetic.
findings.title: FND-002 — AI-DP-01.1

findings.cause: The service trusted client-provided confirmation state.
findings.condition: Two missing-confirmation variants executed the harmless tool.
findings.consequence: A crafted client can create unapproved synthetic exception requests.
findings.criteria: AI-AC-01.1 requires independent authorization and explicit confirmation.
findings.severity_rationale: Authorization integrity is materially affected despite the deliberately harmless tool scope.
findings.title: FND-003 — AI-AC-01.1

findings.cause: The release gate checked only that an artifact existed.
findings.condition: The deployed prompt digest differs from the evaluated digest.
findings.consequence: An untested behavioral change can reach the demonstration environment.
findings.criteria: AI-TE-01.1 requires a passing fixed evaluation for the exact deployed configuration.
findings.severity_rationale: The defect affects change assurance; the system is synthetic and low impact.
findings.title: FND-004 — AI-TE-01.1

limitations: Automated inventory cannot establish business need without owner review.
objectives.objective: Only approved managed identities and reviewer personas are active in project scopes.
objectives.subject_selector: project role assignments and managed identities
objectives.title: Account Management — objective AC-2.1

limitations: Solo-project roles are simulated and are not independent.
objectives.objective: Account ownership and quarterly access review decisions are documented.
objectives.subject_selector: all simulated personas and workload identities
objectives.title: Account Management — objective AC-2.2

limitations: Synthetic test identities do not prove tenant-wide enforcement.
objectives.objective: Wrong identities receive HTTP 403 on private command endpoints.
objectives.title: Access Enforcement — objective AC-3.1

limitations: Reviewer inspection is required for semantic authorization.
objectives.objective: Command authorization design enforces append-only decisions and optimistic concurrency.
objectives.title: Access Enforcement — objective AC-3.2

limitations: Inherited assignments above assessed scopes are reported separately.
objectives.objective: Project role assignments contain no owner-equivalent or wildcard data-plane grants.
objectives.title: Least Privilege — objective AC-6.1

limitations: Attestation does not test effective permission.
objectives.objective: Each workload identity privilege has a documented task need and removal condition.
objectives.subject_selector: assistant, collector, console, deploy, and Sentinel identities
objectives.title: Least Privilege — objective AC-6.2

limitations: Conditional Access policy effectiveness may require tenant-level evidence outside project scope.
objectives.objective: Interactive private-console access requires Entra authentication and MFA-capable policy.
objectives.title: Identification and Authentication (Organizational Users) — objective IA-2.1

limitations: Scanning cannot prove a secret was never disclosed elsewhere.
objectives.objective: Workloads use managed identity or OIDC and repository scans detect no stored credentials.
objectives.title: Authenticator Management — objective IA-5.1

limitations: Configuration evidence does not prove downstream query usefulness.
objectives.objective: Required security, assurance, authentication, tool-use, and configuration-change event categories are configured at every in-scope service.
objectives.subject_selector: Container Apps, Storage, Key Vault, Foundry, API
objectives.title: Event Logging — objective AU-2.1

limitations: Event selection adequacy requires judgment.
objectives.objective: The event taxonomy remains sufficient for stated threats and privacy constraints.
objectives.title: Event Logging — objective AU-2.2

limitations: Reviewer is a simulated persona operated by the project author.
objectives.objective: A reviewer assesses anomalous events, failed collections, and rejected tool escalations.
objectives.title: Audit Record Review, Analysis, and Reporting — objective AU-6.1

limitations: Sampling may miss rare malformed events.
objectives.objective: Correlation IDs, actor pseudonyms, outcomes, and timestamps are present without routine prompt text.
objectives.title: Audit Record Generation — objective AU-12.1

limitations: A successful job does not establish all manual objectives.
objectives.objective: Scheduled and change-triggered assessments run with no false pass on missing evidence.
objectives.title: Continuous Monitoring — objective CA-7.1

limitations: Fourteen-day sample is illustrative, not long-term evidence.
objectives.objective: Monitoring cadence, coverage, freshness, and trend are periodically reviewed.
objectives.title: Continuous Monitoring — objective CA-7.2

limitations: Platform-managed properties are normalized before comparison.
objectives.objective: Deployed resources match compiled, version-controlled infrastructure baselines.
objectives.title: Baseline Configuration — objective CM-2.1

limitations: Public sample replaces live organization identifiers.
objectives.objective: Material changes reference an approved pull request and immutable artifact digest.
objectives.title: Configuration Change Control — objective CM-3.1

limitations: Solo-project approval is a simulated separation of duties.
objectives.objective: Emergency and manual changes are reviewed for authorization and rollback readiness.
objectives.title: Configuration Change Control — objective CM-3.2

limitations: Student-cost profile intentionally accepts public service endpoints.
objectives.objective: Required secure settings pass deterministic policy and configuration rules.
objectives.subject_selector: Container Apps, Storage, Key Vault, networking
objectives.title: Configuration Settings — objective CM-6.1

limitations: No locked WORM policy is used; evidence is tamper-evident, not tamper-proof.
objectives.objective: Evidence storage versioning, soft delete, retention, and restore verification are enabled.
objectives.title: System Backup — objective CP-9.1

limitations: Tabletop evidence does not demonstrate live multi-party response.
objectives.objective: AI abuse and cloud security events have triage, containment, evidence-preservation, and escalation playbooks.
objectives.title: Incident Handling — objective IR-4.1

limitations: Risk acceptance is simulated and cannot represent independent approval.
objectives.objective: Risks use cause-event-impact statements, 5x5 scoring, treatment, owner, and review dates.
objectives.title: Risk Assessment — objective RA-3.1

limitations: Scanner coverage depends on advisory database freshness.
objectives.objective: Images and dependencies are scanned and critical unresolved vulnerabilities block release.
objectives.title: Vulnerability Monitoring and Scanning — objective RA-5.1

limitations: Passing tests only cover declared cases.
objectives.objective: Unit, contract, negative, security, IaC, and behavioral test gates pass before release.
objectives.title: Developer Testing and Evaluation — objective SA-11.1

limitations: Reviewer and developer are simulated personas.
objectives.objective: Test scope and residual limitations are reviewed for each release.
objectives.title: Developer Testing and Evaluation — objective SA-11.2

limitations: The legacy unattached NSG remains safe but is still reported as a configuration finding.
objectives.objective: No attached in-scope network rule allows Internet RDP or SSH ingress.
objectives.subject_selector: network security groups and attached interfaces
objectives.title: Boundary Protection — objective SC-7.1

limitations: No private endpoints, NAT Gateway, or Azure Firewall are deployed.
objectives.objective: Public endpoints are documented, authenticated, rate-limited, and risk accepted for the cost profile.
objectives.title: Boundary Protection — objective SC-7.2

limitations: Azure platform termination is relied upon for managed endpoints.
objectives.objective: All service endpoints require TLS 1.2 or later and storage rejects insecure transfer.
objectives.title: Transmission Confidentiality and Integrity — objective SC-8.1

limitations: Low-volume fixtures may legitimately yield zero security alerts.
objectives.objective: Security telemetry reaches approved workspaces and required queries return fresh data.
objectives.title: System Monitoring — objective SI-4.1

limitations: Synthetic testing cannot establish production false-positive rates.
objectives.objective: Alert rules cover risky role/NSG change, logging deletion, failed assurance, and repeated tool escalation.
objectives.title: System Monitoring — objective SI-4.2

limitations: User comprehension is not measured in the sample assessment.
objectives.objective: Intended and prohibited uses, owners, risk tolerance, human authority, and user notice are current.
objectives.subject_selector: AI governance policy and user interface
objectives.title: AI Use Governance and Accountability — objective AI-GV-01.1

limitations: All corpus data is synthetic; results do not generalize to arbitrary enterprise content.
objectives.objective: Every retrieved document has provenance, classification, integrity digest, and injection disposition.
objectives.title: Grounding Data Provenance and Protection — objective AI-DP-01.1

limitations: Only one harmless consequential tool is assessed.
objectives.objective: Consequential synthetic tools enforce authorization and explicit confirmation before execution.
objectives.title: AI Tool Authorization and Confirmation — objective AI-AC-01.1

limitations: The fixed synthetic set cannot reveal every emerging behavior.
objectives.objective: Model, prompt, retrieval, or tool changes require a fixed evaluation artifact meeting release thresholds.
objectives.title: AI Evaluation and Release Gate — objective AI-TE-01.1

limitations: Automated schema checks do not establish whether escalation thresholds remain appropriate.
objectives.objective: Telemetry supports abuse, grounding, guardrail, tool, model, and configuration monitoring without routine content logging.
objectives.title: AI Monitoring and Escalation — objective AI-MO-01.1

limitations: No real user or production incident evidence exists.
objectives.objective: AI events have documented severity, escalation, evidence preservation, and model-disable decisions.
objectives.title: AI Monitoring and Escalation — objective AI-MO-01.2

observations.condition: A sanitized unattached NSG contains an inbound Any-to-TCP/3389 allow rule at priority 100.
Six of eight synthetic indirect-prompt-injection cases failed their expected behavior.
Two missing-confirmation negative requests executed the synthetic access-exception tool.
The deployed prompt digest differs from the passing evaluation artifact digest.
The Key Vault diagnostic-settings collector received HTTP 403.

remediations.action: Remove the broad RDP rule through version-controlled infrastructure and verify no attachment.
Delimit retrieved content as untrusted, detect embedded instructions, enforce citations and refusal behavior, and rerun the fixed set.
Bind authorization, actor, tool, argument digest, expiry, and single-use confirmation token server-side.
Fail closed unless deployed and evaluated model, prompt, retrieval, corpus, tool, and guardrail digests match.

risks.statement: Because a broad administrative rule remains in the configuration estate, it may be attached or copied, causing avoidable Internet management exposure.
Because retrieved documents can contain adversarial instructions, the assistant may follow them, reducing grounded-answer integrity.
Because confirmation is trusted from client state, a crafted request may execute the synthetic tool without approval, undermining authorization integrity.
Because the gate accepts an artifact for a different digest, an untested AI configuration may deploy and introduce unknown behavioral regression.

system.boundary: The authorization boundary contains the Canada Central assurance plane and the East US 2 synthetic policy-assistant plane. The existing security workspace and GitHub-hosted source, CI, and registry services are external dependencies observed through scoped interfaces; the public console receives sanitized, signed derivatives only.

classifications.description: Sanitized assessment snapshots, public reports, hashes, and non-sensitive system-boundary metadata.
classifications.handling: Publish only after deterministic sanitization, leak scanning, hash verification, and manifest signing.

classifications.description: Synthetic policy corpus, configuration metadata, and ordinary human-readable workpapers.
classifications.handling: Limit to project participants and approved workload identities; do not substitute it for public sanitization.

classifications.description: Raw operational evidence, reviewer events, private resource metadata, and security configuration.
classifications.handling: Keep in private assurance storage with scoped RBAC, encryption, versioning, and retention controls.

classifications.description: Adversarial prompts, model responses, tool-authorization traces, and controlled evaluation content.
classifications.handling: Use only in approved synthetic evaluations; exclude raw content from routine logs and all public artifacts.

system.data_classification: The system is limited to synthetic policy content. Raw operational evidence and controlled evaluation content remain private; only sanitized assessment records, hashes, and non-sensitive boundary metadata may cross into the public console.

data_flows.data: Synthetic policy documents and integrity manifest
data_flows.destination: Corpus Blob and local FTS5 index
data_flows.protection: TLS, workload identity, and manifest digest verification

data_flows.data: Synthetic policy question and pseudonymous session metadata
data_flows.protection: Entra authentication, TLS, authorization, and rate limits
data_flows.retention: Raw content excluded from routine logs

data_flows.protection: Managed identity or isolated Azure-hosted runtime with request limits
data_flows.retention: Request content retained only for controlled evaluations

data_flows.data: Configuration, role, diagnostic, query, and CI evidence
data_flows.protection: Read-only scoped identities, single-repository GitHub App token, and TLS

data_flows.data: Raw and normalized evidence, results, findings, risks, and lifecycle records
data_flows.protection: Managed identity, encryption, versioning, soft delete, and independent hashes

data_flows.data: Reviewer identity, prior state, decision, rationale, version, and artifact hash
data_flows.protection: Entra authentication, role authorization, and optimistic concurrency

data_flows.data: Sanitized assessment package, reports, evidence metadata, and system record
data_flows.protection: Deterministic sanitization, public-boundary leak tests, artifact hashes, and signed manifest

exclusions: Azure and GitHub provider internals, physical security, and controls unavailable through the scoped service interfaces.
Unrelated tenant resources and any workload outside the declared assessment scope.
Production, personal, customer, regulated, or otherwise non-synthetic data.
Independent audit, certification, regulatory attestation, or organizational separation-of-duties claims.
Enterprise private-networking controls, paid Defender plans, API Management, AKS, Cosmos DB, and other services excluded by the CAD 25 monthly student-cost profile.
Automatic remediation, risk acceptance, exception approval, or finding closure without a new evidence-backed retest and simulated reviewer decision.

identities.name: Bootstrap deployment identity
identities.privilege: Project bootstrap scope only; separated from runtime collection.
identities.purpose: Create workload identities, federation, and approved role assignments.

identities.name: GitHub deployment identity
identities.purpose: Deploy immutable workload revisions and version-controlled resources.

identities.assigned_scope: Synthetic corpus and selected model deployment
identities.name: Policy assistant identity
identities.privilege: Corpus read and model invocation only.
identities.purpose: Read the synthetic corpus and invoke the selected model endpoint.

identities.assigned_scope: Declared assessed scopes and private assurance stores
identities.authentication: User-assigned managed identity and short-lived GitHub App installation token
identities.name: Assurance collector identity
identities.privilege: Read assessed scopes, ingest assurance telemetry, sign digests, and write evidence; no remediation permission.
identities.purpose: Collect read-only configuration and operational evidence and publish private packages.

identities.assigned_scope: Assurance public evidence and command store
identities.name: Assurance API identity
identities.privilege: Derived evidence read and command-table append only.
identities.purpose: Read derived assessment records and append command and review events.

identities.name: Sentinel content identity
identities.privilege: Security-content management without workload deployment or role grants.
identities.purpose: Deploy version-controlled analytics and workbook content.

inventory.lifecycle: External dependency scoped to one repository
inventory.name: GitHub source, Actions, and GHCR
inventory.type: External source control, CI, and image registry

inventory.lifecycle: Pre-existing and not owned by this project
inventory.name: Existing security workspace

system.name: Azure AI Continuous Assurance
system.shared_responsibility: Microsoft operates Azure physical infrastructure and managed-service control planes; GitHub operates its source-control, Actions, and registry services. The AICA project owns service selection and configuration, workload code, identity and access design, data classification and minimization, evidence collection, deterministic evaluation, simulated human review, incident handling, and honest reporting of limitations. Provider internals are not assessed.
trust_boundaries: The public console is untrusted and read-only; it receives no Azure credential or private resource identifier.
Private reviewer commands cross Entra authentication and server-side role authorization before an append-only decision is queued.
The collector crosses assessed Azure, GitHub, and security-plane boundaries with distinct read-only credentials and writes only to private assurance storage.
Retrieved corpus text crosses an AI data boundary and is always treated as untrusted input.
Model-requested tools cross an independent authorization boundary; model output never grants authority.
Private evidence crosses a deterministic sanitization boundary before public release, and private and public forms receive independent hashes.
The assessment job crosses a signing boundary to a non-exportable P-256 key and records the exact public-key fingerprint in each manifest.
